Title: E1-S1 Provision Supabase Storage bucket + RLS policies
Description: Create a `ticket-attachments` bucket on Supabase Storage. Service-role write, signed-URL read scoped by user-role + acceptance gate. Default visibility: private. Set max file size 25 MB; allowed MIME types: image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp, image/heic, video/mp4 (no preview yet), audio/ogg.

Title: E1-S2 Telegram → Supabase storage download/upload pipeline
Description: Add `services/attachmentService.ts` with `downloadFromTelegramAndStore(file_id, ticket_id, org_id) → { storage_path, mime_type, size }`. Uses Bot API `getFile` → fetch bytes → `supabase.storage.from('ticket-attachments').upload(path, bytes)`. Returns canonical path like `org/<org_id>/ticket/<ticket_id>/<uuid>.<ext>`.

Title: E1-S3 Persist canonical path + metadata in ticket_attachments
Description: On attachment insert (currently in `services/telegramFlow.ts` and `app/api/webhooks/telegram/route.ts`), call the new pipeline and store the canonical path in `ticket_attachments.storage_path`. Move the Telegram file_id into `metadata_json.telegram_file_id` for audit.

Title: E1-S4 Inline image preview in ticket detail page
Description: Add an Attachments section to `app/(dashboard)/tickets/[id]/page.tsx`. Renders inline thumbnails for image MIME types; falls back to a download link for non-images. Visibility gate: privileged roles always; ground_worker only after `citizen_identity_revealed_at`; leaders see metadata only.

Title: E1-S5 Backfill existing telegram: pointers
Description: One-time script `scripts/backfill-telegram-attachments.ts`. Reads all `ticket_attachments` where `storage_path LIKE 'telegram:%'`, resolves and uploads each via the new pipeline, rewrites the storage_path. Idempotent. Handles Telegram's 1-year file_id expiry by skipping with a warning.

Title: E1-S6 Retention policy + cleanup cron
Description: Per-org retention column (`organization_settings.attachment_retention_months`, default 24). Cron deletes storage objects older than the window. Defer until post-launch if W3 timing is tight.

Title: E2-S1 Extend Amplify source schema to include note_ids[]
Description: Add `note_ids: string[]` to the `amplify_sessions.metadata_json` shape. No migration needed (JSONB column). Update TypeScript types in `types/database.ts` (or wherever AmplifySession is typed).

Title: E2-S2 Extend Amplify source schema to include attachment_ids[]
Description: Add `attachment_ids: string[]` to the `amplify_sessions.metadata_json` shape. Same channel as S1.

Title: E2-S3 AmplifyEditor UI — note checklist on the left pane
Description: In `components/amplify/AmplifyEditor.tsx`, fetch ticket notes (non-soft-deleted) and render a checkbox list under Sources. Show author full name + timestamp + first ~80 chars of note text. Selection state persists to the session via PATCH /api/amplify/sessions/[id].

Title: E2-S4 AmplifyEditor UI — attachment thumbnail picker
Description: Same component. Pull `ticket_attachments` filtered to image MIMEs. Click to toggle selected; selected count badge on the section header. Uses the same signed-URL flow as E1-S4.

Title: E2-S5 Prompt builder — embed selected note text into context
Description: Update `services/amplifyService.ts` `generateAmplifyContent` to read `note_ids` from the session, fetch the note rows, and inline their `body_text` into the prompt's sources block. Cap total note text to ~3000 chars; if exceeded, prefer notes marked `is_internal=false` first, then newest internals.

Title: E2-S6 Prompt builder — pass image URLs as references
Description: Same service. For each `attachment_id` in the session, fetch a long-lived signed URL and pass into the prompt as `[Image: <url>] — caption: <attachment.caption or 'photo from citizen'>`. The generated draft can reference them as `[Image 1]`, `[Image 2]` etc. and central support arranges in their CMS.

Title: E2-S7 Audit log — record selected note + attachment IDs
Description: Extend the `amplify_content_generated` audit event payload with `note_ids: string[]` and `attachment_ids: string[]`. Helps trace which materials seeded a public-facing draft.

Title: E3-S1 Read intake_conversation_version per inbound message
Description: In `app/api/webhooks/telegram/route.ts`, call `getIntakeVersion(ORG_ID)` (from `services/intakeSettingsService`) before dispatching to the conversation engine. Cache in-memory for 30s to avoid hammering Supabase.

Title: E3-S2 V2 dispatch path in Telegram webhook
Description: If version=v2, call `processInbound({history, newMessage, existingDraft})` from `services/intakeConversationManager`. Send the `replyText` back to Telegram via the existing `sendTelegramMessage`. Persist the citizen message + bot reply into `channel_conversations.metadata_json.history[]`.

Title: E3-S3 Persist draftUpdates cumulatively per conversation
Description: Merge `draftUpdates` from the response into `channel_conversations.metadata_json.draft`. Pass the merged draft as `existingDraft` on the next inbound. Reset `draft` after a ticket is filed.

Title: E3-S4 File the ticket when readyToFile=true (in_scope)
Description: Reuse `services/ticketService.createTicket` with the accumulated draft. Send the citizen a confirmation message in their language (use the bot's replyText for the file-confirmation turn, OR a follow-up message generated by `processInbound` with the new ticket_number).

Title: E3-S5 File needs-review ticket flagged for human review
Description: Same as S4 but ensure `needs_triage=true` and the ticket gets a `needs_review_by_intake` audit-log row noting `scopeReason`. Central support sees it in their queue. Bot tells citizen 'we're looking into it'.

Title: E3-S6 Per-chat allowlist for staged V2 rollout
Description: Env var `V2_INTAKE_ALLOWLIST=chat_id1,chat_id2`. Webhook checks this; if non-empty, V2 only runs for chat_ids in the list (regardless of org setting). If empty, org setting alone decides. Drop the env var once V2 is fully trusted.

Title: E4-S1 Provision new Supabase project
Description: Create JTG-prod Supabase project. Copy URL + anon key + service role key.

Title: E4-S2 Provision new Vercel project
Description: Connect Vercel to the same `Abhijit-sai/vocal-app` repo. Configure project name, env vars to be set in S6, domain (TBD).

Title: E4-S3 Provision new Clerk app
Description: Create JTG Clerk application (or sub-env on existing account). Copy publishable + secret keys. Configure sign-in/up redirect URLs to the JTG Vercel host.

Title: E4-S4 Register @JTG_citizen_bot via @BotFather
Description: Create the bot, set bio + commands. Save the bot token. Generate a 64-char hex webhook secret. Update `TENANT_CONFIG.bots.citizen.username` in `config/tenant.config.ts` on the JTG branch.

Title: E4-S5 Register @JTG_worker_bot via @BotFather
Description: Same as S4 for the worker bot. Update `TENANT_CONFIG.bots.worker.username`.

Title: E4-S6 Set env vars in Vercel + register webhooks
Description: 12 env vars (Supabase × 3, Clerk × 4, Telegram × 4, Org × 1). Use the existing checklist from .env.local.example. After deploy, call setWebhook on each bot pointing at the JTG Vercel host.

Title: E4-S7 Apply migrations 001-006 to JTG Supabase
Description: Paste each migration SQL into the Supabase SQL editor in order. Verify the row counts after each.

Title: E4-S8 Run seed:telangana-tier-a against JTG
Description: Local: `ORG_ID=<jtg_org> npm run seed:telangana-tier-a`. Creates 165 rows: 1 state + 33 districts + 119 ACs + 13 mandals (Rajanna Sircilla).

Title: E4-S9 Run Sircilla village seed (Tier B)
Description: The 117 villages are already inside `scripts/data/telangana/mandals/rajanna-sircilla.json` under each mandal's `villages[]`. The same seed runner picks them up — no separate step needed once the script's village loop is verified.

Title: E4-S10 Seed Sircilla-specific local gov contacts
Description: User to source the list: MLA office, MROs (5 mandals), tehsildars, ward members, police circle inspectors, electricity-board feeders, etc. Drop into a CSV; extend `seed-gov-contacts.ts` to accept a `--csv=path` argument; run against JTG DB.

Title: E4-S11 Bootstrap super_admin user
Description: Create Clerk user → manually insert matching `users` row in JTG Supabase with `role_id` = super_admin UUID, `organization_id` = JTG org. First sign-in via Clerk binds the `clerk_user_id`.

Title: E4-S12 Bootstrap central_support team (2-3 users)
Description: Same pattern as S11. Hand over the Clerk invite + first-time-login flow to the operators.

Title: E4-S13 Bootstrap initial ground_workers (5-10)
Description: Same pattern. Each worker also gets `user_territories` rows mapping them to specific Sircilla AC wards/villages they cover. CSV-driven script to bulk-create.

Title: E4-S14 Smoke-test end-to-end on Telegram
Description: Real citizen Telegram account → send to @JTG_citizen_bot → confirm ticket created in JTG DB → confirm assigned worker received the alert on @JTG_worker_bot → accept → status updates → close. Repeat with a needs_review case + an out_of_scope case if V2 is on.

Title: E4-S15 Soft-launch with limited initial cohort
Description: First 24h: only seeded test citizens. Then open to ~50 real users sourced via JTG ground team. Monitor /audit + Supabase logs hourly.

Title: E4-S16 Operational runbook + worker training material
Description: One-pager for central support: how to triage, when to use Amplify, how to handle a needs_review ticket. Short video (5 min) for ground workers: how to link Telegram, how to accept, how to update status, how to upload a photo.

Title: E5-S1 Mandal data for the other 32 districts
Description: Per-district JSON files following the `rajanna-sircilla.json` schema. Source from Wikipedia + Telangana revenue. Drop into `scripts/data/telangana/mandals/`. Re-running the seed picks them up.

Title: E5-S2 GHMC ward-level data for Hyderabad
Description: 150 wards, each under the correct AC. Important because civic complaint volume in Hyderabad will be high relative to other districts.

Title: E5-S3 Village-level data for non-Sircilla mandals
Description: Long tail. Only add when a new constituency client signs and needs ward-level accuracy.

Title: E5-S4 CSV import pipeline for non-developer onboarders
Description: UI under /admin/territories that lets central support drop a CSV and run the seed without code changes. Stretches into the Territory Admin UI epic.

Title: E6-S1 Clerk → Supabase JWT integration + flip pages to RLS
Description: Configure JWT template in Clerk pointing at Supabase. Enable third-party auth in Supabase dashboard. Migrate `/tickets`, `/my-assignments`, `/triage`, `/dashboard`, `/directory` to use `createSupabaseUserClient()` one at a time. Verify each role's access.

Title: E6-S2 Territory Admin UI
Description: Tree view, create/edit/delete nodes, set centroids. Replaces CSV/JSON workflow. Lives at /admin/territories. Super_admin + central_support.

Title: E6-S3 External cron for expire-assignments
Description: Replace `/jobs` manual button. Set up cron-job.org or Upstash QStash to hit `/api/cron/expire-assignments` every minute with a CRON_SECRET header.

Title: E6-S4 External cron for worker daily reminders
Description: Same pattern. Hit `/api/cron/worker-reminders` once per morning per org.

Title: E6-S5 Sentry / error tracking
Description: Add @sentry/nextjs. Client + server SDKs. Sample rate 10%. Tag releases.

Title: E6-S6 Reports CSV / XLSX export
Description: All report views downloadable. Audit-logged. Restricted by role + territory.

Title: E6-S7 Worker leaderboards on /reports
Description: Resolution count, avg time-to-resolution, acceptance ratio, SLA-met rate. Tabular + sortable.

Title: E6-S8 Mobile-responsive tables
Description: Card-view at <md breakpoint for /tickets, /triage, /workers. They currently overflow horizontally on phones.

Title: E6-S9 WhatsApp adapter via Gupshup or Wati
Description: Adapter plugs into intakeConversationManager (it's channel-agnostic). BSP setup, webhook, template approvals. ~1 week including business verification.

Title: E6-S10 Apply migration 006 + verify V1↔V2 switch
Description: User action: paste 006 SQL into Supabase SQL editor for both demo and JTG projects. Verify /admin/intake-settings save now works without error.

Title: E7-S1 Voice transcription preprocessing via Gemini multimodal
Description: Bot downloads voice note via Telegram getFile → sends to Gemini → extracts transcript + detected language → places into `IntakeRequest.newMessage.media.voice_transcript`.

Title: E7-S2 Image vision description via Gemini multimodal
Description: Bot pipes image URL to Gemini Vision → description + extracted-text from any signs/documents → into `media.image_description`.

Title: E7-S3 Wire media preprocessing into webhook V2 path
Description: Runs BEFORE `processInbound()` so the LLM sees the enriched context. Time budget: 8s max for voice, 5s for image. If timeout, fall through to text-only with a warning.

Title: E7-S4 Sarvam AI fallback for Telugu/Tinglish voice
Description: Optional quality upgrade. Only triggered when Gemini's transcript confidence is low. Adds a new vendor account.

Title: E8-S1 Stand up AWS RDS Postgres for JTG production
Description: Multi-AZ, daily automated backups, parameter group tuned. Security group restricting access to the ECS/EC2 cluster.

Title: E8-S2 Self-host Supabase OSS stack on ECS or EC2
Description: Docker Compose: PostgREST + Studio + GoTrue (disabled — Clerk handles auth) + Storage. Zero application code change.

Title: E8-S3 Migrate JTG data via pg_dump / pg_restore
Description: One-shot cutover. Plan ~30 min downtime. Verify row counts. Roll back if anything mismatches.

Title: E8-S4 Move Next.js app from Vercel to AWS
Description: Run `next start` on AWS App Runner or ECS. Set up EventBridge for cron jobs (replaces external cron). Configure domain + TLS.

Title: E8-S5 Wire CloudWatch + alerts
Description: Replace Vercel's observability. Tail application logs, RDS slow-query logs, basic up/down checks.
